folio.label: 133v
folio.l: To ma~nes sigút , that alle the Rokkes blake
Of Britaygne , were I voided euäychon
And Shippes by the brynke , come and gon
And in swicú fourme , endure , a day or two
Thanne were my brother warisshede , of his woo
Thanne must she nedes holden , hire byheste
Or ellis he shal shame hire , atte the leste
¢ What shulde I make , a lengere tale of this
Vn to his brothers bed , he comen is
And swicú confort , he ©af hi~ for to gon
To Orliens , that he vp stirte a noon
And on his wey fortúward , tha~ne is he fare
In hope for to ben lissed , of his care
¢ Whan they were come , almoost to that Citee
But if it were , a two forlongö or three
A ©onge Clerk romyngö , by hiö self , they mette
Whicú that in latyn , thriftily hem grette
And after that he seide , a wonder thyngö
I knowe q¶ he the cause , of ©oure comyngö
And er they ferthere , any foote wente
He told hem al that was , in here entente
¢ This Britoun Clerk , hi~ asked of felawes
The whicú that he had knowe , in olde dawes
And he answered hi~ , that they deede were
For whicú he wep~ ful ofte , many a teere
Doun of his hors , Aurelius ligút a noon
And witú this Magicien , fortú he is goon
Hom to his hous , and maden he~ wel attese
Hem lakked no vitaille , that mygút he~ plese
So wel arrayed hous , as there was oon
Aurelius in his lyf , saw neuäe noon
He shewed hi~ , er he wente to soupere
Forestes Parkes , ful of wylde dere
There saw he hertes , witú here hornes heye
The grettest , that euäe was seyn , witú eye
He saw of hem , an hundred slayn witú houndes
And some witú arwes blede , of bittere woundes
¢ HHHHHe saw whan voided were , the wilde deere
Theise faucons , vp on a fair Ryuere
That witú here haukes , han the heroun slayn
Tho saw he knygútes , Iustyngö in a pleyn
And after this , he dide hi~ swicú plesaunce
That he hi~ shewed , his lady on a daunce
On whicú , hi~ self he daunced , as hi~ thougút
